Wispr Flow: Dictation That Works Everywhere

What do you think? …

Chris Messina
Raycast

Raycast

Impressive results for all Pro and Pro Max iPhones from series 12, and all non-Pro iPhones from series 14...!

Reminds me of @Creaceed 's Hydra 2.

Here's how it works: [...] Reminds me of @Creaceed 's Hydra 2.

Here's how it works:

"A simplified depiction of Indigo's image processing pipeline. The frontend (at left) aligns and merges a stream of raw frames flowing from the camera. The merged but still (nearly) raw image is optionally stored as an Adobe DNG (Digital Negative) file (red box at top). The look computed by our Adaptive Color Profile AI (green box) is stored in the DNG file alongside the pixels, but doesn't change them. The output of this AI also drives the backend (at right), which consists of tone mapping, sharpening, and color adjustments. These operations do change the pixel values. The modified pixels are compressed and stored in the JPEG file. The baking of adjustments into pixels is why editing a JPEG is always harder than editing a DNG." [...] Ritwik Sengupta

Now this is something I will love to try on my new iPhone 16 Plus!!! Let's see how things pan out!!!

Stryker

Stryker

Lazar Radenovic

Sealit

New User

I discovered Stryker way too late!! I was fond of using Zanti on Android Lollipop 10 years ago...

By the time I discovered Csploit and Dsploit Android had already surpassed version 13 so the old apps don't work anymore and soon enough Zanti became obsolete as well.

I found Stryker in 2024 and I was pleasantly surprised I could still do some of the cool things I could do with Zanti.

Casual users just need an Nmap, Bettercap, Hydra, John the ripper, and Metasploit Framework all in one. application!

I am sad to see Stryker no longer supported. [...] Stryker
